manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

berth

Related words are:

- dock
- port
- position

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue, following format CLUE: <some text>. Must avoid using target word "berth" or any of its morphological variants (e.g., "berths", "berthing") and also must avoid related words: dock, port, position. Also can't use those words or morphological variants. Need to describe "berth". Need to be concise.

We need to think of a clue that hints at berth without using those words. Can't say "dock" or "port" etc. Could say "sleeping place on a ship". But "sleeping place" could be "berth". That's okay; we aren't using the word itself. That's allowed. We just can't use the target word itself. "Sleeping place on a vessel" is okay, but "vessel" is fine. Must avoid "dock", "port", "position". So "sleeping place on a vessel" is fine.

We need short description: maybe "A ship's sleeping spot". That hints at berth.

Make sure not to include any of the related words. "sleeping spot" is fine. Could also use "railroad sleeping car seat" but that might be ambiguous. Better to be clear: "A ship's sleeping spot". That's short.

Thus output: CLUE: A ship's sleeping spot.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to guess a target word that another player describes to you.

You can make one guess at each trial. You win when you guess the target word. You lose when you cannot guess it in 3 tries.

After each trial you will get a new hint from the other player which starts with CLUE.

Make your guesses by just saying the word using the following form: GUESS: <a word>

Let us start.


CLUE: A ship's sleeping spot.

message.reasoning:
The role: we are ChatGPT playing a word guessing game. We need to guess the target word based on clues. Each guess we output "GUESS:word>". We have 3 tries total. First clue: "A ship's sleeping spot." We must interpret: on a ship, sleeping spot could be "cabin", "berth". "Berth" is a sleeping spot for a sailor on a ship. Could also be "bunk". Among those, "berth" is a place to sleep, also used for the ship's docking location but also sleeping. Likely target word is "berth".

We have 3 tries. Let's guess "BERTH".
